bugs_and_notes: LATENT (not triggered): m5_20_2_a_eom.py:172-173 grad_static_4 default w4 uses module globals NR,NZ instead of Mnp.shape; wrong for any non-128x256 grid.

NORMALIZATION NOTE: kin_form_apply (m5_20_2_a_eom.py:214-224) uses T = sum_i <F,F>_eta (adjoint factor 2), 4x smaller than the H-consistent factor-4 convention of u_eta/k_eff; rank/negativity census unaffected (diagnostic only), but kin_census max|eig| magnitudes are 4x low vs the H normalization.

COSMETIC: m5_20_2_b_dynamics.py:168-170 envelope_growth_rate for the rotation run fits log(max(0,1e-30)): the quoted '+1.5e-16/t' is the artifact of fitting a constant floor; the underlying stability statement is solid (time-mixing identically zero, a12 bounded 0.0194-0.0195, |E dev| 2.2e-6 abs over T=400).
COSMETIC: omega_peak (0.2856) reported for the DIVERGED T2_clock/T3_boost4b runs is an FFT over the truncated pre-NaN window; meaningless, not load-bearing.

PRESENTATION: method note II.1 finding 5 quotes K_eff ranges that cover the three primary states while the sentence includes the alternate branch (whose rows reach +3.3e8 rot_J13 / -2.5e8 boost_B01); sign claim itself holds on all 24 rows.
CURIOSITY (not load-bearing): T2_clock and T3_boost4b trajectories report bitwise-identical maxabs from t=20.5 onward despite genuinely different initial states; late-blowup saturation artifact.

INTERPRETATION CAVEAT (C3 control): on the J-commuting uniform background diag(-8,.7,.7,.2) the same (0,1) bump does NOT run away over T=60 (time-mixing amplitude never grows above the injected 0.02). The measured runaway therefore involves the A_phi equivariant background of the axisym vac4 representation, i.e. it is a statement about states with gradients (as the K_eff census implies), not about time-mixing bumps per se.
